notes: Integrated from the real Grade 2 Quarter 1, Book 1 source file.
Early card: shorter text, short sentences, easiest independent start.

book.title: The Three Little Kittens
book.description: The Three Little Kittens includes a six-chapter reading sequence, vocabulary, comprehension questions, journal/writing prompts, and assessment checks.

choiceGuidance.reason: shorter text, short sentences, easiest independent start

weeks.title: The Three Little Kittens Book Card
vocabulary: 1. growl - to make a low, rumbling sound (like a hungry stomach)
1. remembered - brought back to mind; recalled
2. proudly - with pride; showing you feel good about something
1. basin - a bowl-shaped container for holding water
journal: Write about a time you lost something important. What steps did you take to find it?
Explain how the kittens change from the beginning to the end of the story. Use two details from the text.
writing: Write about a time you lost something important. What steps did you take to find it?
Explain how the kittens change from the beginning to the end of the story. Use two details from the text.
questions: 1. What are the names of the three kittens?
2. Why was Mother Cat upset with the kittens?
1. Why were the kittens' tummies growling?
2. Where did the kittens look first for their mittens?
1. Where did the kittens find their mittens?
1. How did Mother Cat feel when the kittens found their mittens?
2. What did Mother Cat promise to give the kittens?
1. How did the kittens' mittens get dirty?
2. What was the pie made of?
1. What did the kittens use to wash their mittens?
2. What lesson did the kittens learn?
assessment: Use chapter comprehension questions for daily checks.
Use journal/writing prompt as written-response evidence.
